sections.paragraph:
ὁμόσε (ὁμός ‘one and the same, common’) adv. (Hom. et al.; pap) to one and the same place, also for ὁμοῦ=together (Polyb. 6, 7, 5 al.; Vett. Val. ind.; PGiss 4, 6 [II a.d.]; Jos., Ant. 12, 292.—B-D-F §103; Rob. 299f) ὁμόσε ὄντων αὐτῶν Ac 20:18 v.l.—DELG s.v. ὁμό.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ind. ind. = index

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 709
